item.title: U.S. launches 7th straight day of Iran strikes as Strait of Hormuz freezes up
item.description: As the U.S. and Iran escalate attacks 140 days into the war, an industry analyst says "nobody is willing to move" through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Trump claims China compromised voter data that's already publicly available
item.description: One of President Trump's biggest claims in his Thursday night election security speech was that China accessed more than 200 million Americans' voter information. But, as CBS News executive director of elections and surveys Anthony Salvanto explains, it's already publicly available.

item.title: Trump sows doubt about elections in primetime speech
item.description: In a primetime speech on Thursday night, President Trump baselessly claimed the U.S. is beset by catastrophic election vulnerabilities. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe, Anthony Salvanto and David Becker join to unpack the president's remarks.

item.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for July 19, 2026
item.description: This week's guests include Democratic Sen. Mark Warner of Virginia, New York Gov. Kathy Hochul and White House border czar Tom Homan.

item.title: Chip sector losses drag markets lower
item.description: The semiconductor industry experienced losses for its third day on Friday, dragging markets even lower. CBS News contributor Javier David has more.

item.title: Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.description: Taylor Farms said none of its branded salads or kits contain the iceberg lettuce associated with the outbreak.

item.title: Maps show wildfire smoke forecast, air quality alerts in swath of U.S.
item.description: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and the U.S. East Coast this week.

item.title: Journalist targeted by Iran says U.K.'s crackdown on IRGC is "the happiest day"
item.description: British Iranian journalist Pouria Zeraati was stabbed in what is believed to have been an Iranian state-sponsored attack in 2024.

item.title: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott says Uvalde "hit very, very hard" by flooding
item.description: Gov. Greg Abbott gave an update Friday on Texas' response to deadly flooding in the state's central and southern regions.

item.title: Taylor Farms voluntarily pulls iceberg lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.description: Taylor Farms on Friday said it was voluntarily pulling its iceberg lettuce after it was linked to a nationwide cyclosporiasis outbreak. CBS News correspondent Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

item.title: Trump threatens Canada with higher tariffs over wildfire smoke
item.description: President Trump on Friday called the smoke from wildfires in Canada trickling into U.S. airspace unacceptable, saying he would hold the country responsible. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has the latest.

item.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak traced to lettuce from Mexico used by Taco Bell
item.description: A lettuce supplier to fast-food giant Taco Bell has been linked to a nationwide cyclosporiasis outbreak that has sickened thousands of people, the CDC said.

item.title: Trump encourages Darline Graham to run for Lindsey Graham's seat
item.description: Darline Graham indicated in a White House meeting Thursday that she's weighing a bid, CBS News confirmed. A day later, President Trump wrote on Truth Social: "RUN, DARLINE, RUN! "

item.title: Newsom's "competitive bid" diaper deal wasn't. California's budget includes dozens of similar no-bid exemptions.
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom said the $6.2 million Baby2Baby contract "went through a competitive bidding process." State records reveal it didn't. CBS California Investigates found dozens of similar no-bid deals in the state budget worth over $1 billion.

item.title: Strong earthquake hits Mexico-Guatemala border, tsunami alert lifted
item.description: The U.S. Geological Survey reported that the earthquake had a magnitude of 7.3 with the epicenter 30 miles southwest of Aquiles Serdan, Mexico.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 19)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: China calls Trump's election interference claims "pure fabrication"
item.description: A spokesman for the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs denied President Trump's claims of election interference in the 2020 election. CBS News foreign correspondent Anna Coren reports.

item.title: Have U.S. and Iran negotiations reached a standstill as strikes continue?
item.description: Jonathan Wachtel, a global affairs analyst and a former spokesperson for the U.S. Mission to the United Nations, joins to discuss where negotiations stand as the U.S. and Iran continue to exchange attacks for the sixth night in a row.

item.title: DHS secretary Mullin claims 250,000 noncitizens are registered to vote in the U.S.
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin claims that there are 250,000 noncitizens registered to vote in the U.S. CBS News' Nicole Sganga has more.

item.title: Most Americans don't think there's widespread voter fraud, CBS News poll shows
item.description: President Trump spoke extensively about election integrity during his primetime speech, alleging, without evidence, that China intervened in the 2020 election. CBS News executive director of elections and surveys Anthony Salvanto breaks down the latest polling on what Americans believe.

item.title: Novak Djokovic talks tennis career, aging in the sport and Serena Williams' return
item.description: Novak Djokovic talks to "CBS Mornings" about still competing at 39 years old, the U.S. Open and Serena Williams' return to the court.

item.title: ICE officer in Maine shooting has history of violent behavior, relatives say
item.description: The Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer who shot and killed a Colombian man in Maine this week is an Army veteran who has struggled with serious mental health issues since early childhood, relatives say.

item.title: Adidas CEO, soccer legend Marcelo on World Cup final, "Trionda Final" ball and sport's impact
item.description: Argentina and Spain will face off in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final on Sunday. Adidas CEO Bjørn Gulden and Brazilian soccer legend Marcelo join "CBS News 24/7 Mornings" as football fever reaches new heights.

item.title: Boomer wealth transfer will mostly benefit the already rich, report finds
item.description: Trillions of wealth passed down by the baby boomers will likely go to younger Americans who are already rich. Here's how much they'll get.

item.title: Coca-Cola temporarily halts production of Fairlife milk after cyberattack
item.description: A cybersecurity incident has forced Coca-Cola to suspend Fairlife milk production in the U.S. An investigation is underway.

item.title: Appeals court keeps in place Pentagon's escort policy for reporters
item.description: The appeals court in Washington, D.C., divided 2-1 in ruling in favor of the Trump administration in the legal battle over the Pentagon's escort policy for journalists.

item.title: "Master yachtsman" fugitive captured on boat after 21 years on the run
item.description: Ronald L. Fischer, who was featured on "America's Most Wanted," fled Rhode Island during his criminal trial for first-degree sexual assault in 2005.

item.title: Fact-checking Trump's speech on election security
item.description: President Trump delivered a speech on election security Thursday night at the White House. Here are the facts behind some of his claims.

item.title: Trump revisits disputed claims about elections but offers no new proof of fraud
item.description: In a primetime address, President Trump alleged the U.S. election system falls "catastrophically short," revisiting a topic that has drawn his attention for years — and making claims that election experts have heavily disputed.

item.title: Tillis says he won't support Blanche unless he meets with Epstein survivors
item.description: Sen. Thom Tillis said that Todd Blanche must meet with survivors of convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein's crimes before he'll vote to advance his nomination for attorney general out of the Senate Judiciary Committee.
